questions.question: What did the 2024 meta-analysis of 12 RCTs (707 participants) find regarding probiotics and depression?

options.text: Probiotics had no measurable effect on depressive symptoms
Probiotics produced a statistically significant reduction in depressive symptoms with a small-to-moderate effect size
Only single-strain probiotics were effective
Probiotics were only effective at doses above 100 billion CFU

questions.explanation: The 2024 meta-analysis found that probiotic supplementation produced a statistically significant reduction in depressive symptoms compared to placebo, with a small-to-moderate effect size. Eight weeks emerged as a minimum threshold for reliable mood effects, with 10^9-10^10 CFU daily as the effective dose range.

questions.question: Why are probiotic effects described as 'strain-specific' rather than 'species-specific'?

options.text: All strains within a species have identical effects
The species name determines the therapeutic effect, not the strain
Different strains within the same species (e.g., L. rhamnosus GG vs. L. rhamnosus CCFM1228) have entirely different clinical properties
Strain designations are just marketing labels with no scientific basis

questions.explanation: Probiotic effects are strain-specific, meaning that L. rhamnosus strain GG has entirely different clinical properties than L. rhamnosus strain CCFM1228, despite sharing a species name. Products listing only the species (e.g., 'Lactobacillus acidophilus' without a strain designation) cannot be verified for any specific mental health outcome.

questions.question: What is L. rhamnosus CCFM1228's primary psychobiotic mechanism?

options.text: It destroys harmful bacteria in the gut
It produces GABA directly in the gut, increases vagal nerve tone, and modulates the HPA axis stress response
It increases serotonin reuptake in the brain
It reduces appetite and promotes weight loss

questions.explanation: L. rhamnosus CCFM1228 produces GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) directly in the gut, increases vagal nerve tone, and modulates the HPA axis stress response. It is one of the few strains with a clear, documented mechanism connecting gut colonization to brain neurochemistry.

questions.question: According to the 2025 prebiotics vs. probiotics review, what is the key difference in their mental health effects?

options.text: Prebiotics are effective while probiotics are not
Probiotics have stronger evidence for direct mood improvement, while prebiotics have stronger evidence for cortisol reduction and stress reactivity
There is no difference between prebiotics and probiotics
Prebiotics work faster than probiotics for depression

questions.explanation: The 2025 review found that probiotics have stronger evidence for direct mood improvement (through strain-specific mechanisms like GABA production and HPA axis modulation), while prebiotics have stronger evidence for cortisol reduction and stress reactivity (by supporting endogenous beneficial bacteria that produce short-chain fatty acids). Combining them (synbiotics) may yield superior outcomes.

questions.question: What minimum duration of probiotic supplementation is needed for reliable mood effects?

options.text: 2-3 days
1 week
8 weeks
6 months

questions.explanation: Eight weeks emerged as a minimum threshold for reliable mood effects in the meta-analysis. The effective dose range is 10^9-10^10 CFU daily, and consistency matters more than mega-dosing intermittently. The JAMA Psychiatry pilot trial also used an 8-week duration, with effects observable by week 4 but continuing to strengthen through week 8.

title: Probiotic Protocols: Evidence-Based Strain Selection - Quiz
